title: Italian Wedding Soup
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no meaningful cholesterol contributors. The broth is vegetable-based and the meatballs are explicitly plant-based, while the barley, vegetables, kale, olive oil, lemon zest, and herbs contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the soup is built around vegetables, greens, barley, and plant-based protein, making it fiber-forward and supportive of LDL lowering. Flavor comes from aromatics, broth, herbs, and lemon rather than saturated fat.
intro: Italian Wedding Soup comes from the Italian concept of “minestra maritata,” or “married soup,” where greens and meat come together in balance rather than referring to weddings themselves. This version follows that tradition with a clear, aromatic broth, tender grains, and hearty meatballs, creating a rustic, comforting soup rooted in Southern Italian home cooking.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Barley
items: In a small saucepan, combine the pearl barley and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook until tender, about 25–30 minutes. Drain any excess water and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Build the Aromatic Base
items: In a soup pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion, carrot, celery, and a pinch of salt. Cook until softened, about 6–7 minutes. Stir in the garlic and oregano and cook for 1 minute more to bloom the aromatics.

stepGroups.title: Simmer the Broth
items: Add the veggie broth to the pot and bring to a gentle simmer.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Meatballs
items: Add the meatballs to the pot and simmer for 10–12 minutes, following package directions if they differ, until heated through and integrated into the broth.

stepGroups.title: Add the Greens and Barley
items: Stir in the chopped kale and the cooked barley. Simmer for another 5 minutes until the greens are wilted and the soup is cohesive.

stepGroups.title: Finish and Serve
items: Add the lemon zest, parsley, black pepper, and adjust salt to taste. Ladle into bowls, ensuring each serving contains greens, barley, and meatballs.
